tasks.title: recommendedEffort returns effort=7 for unknown hardware concurrency (hwc=0), but hwc=0 means unknown not zero-core
tasks.reason_for_tier: category 'inverted conditional / wrong default' defaults to sonnet (standard local work)

tasks.title: recommendedQualitySearch returns 'full' for MT tiers with unknown hardware concurrency (hwc=0), inconsistent with effort heuristic intent
tasks.reason_for_tier: category 'inverted conditional / wrong default' defaults to sonnet (standard local work)

tasks.title: isSimdProbe and isRelaxedProbe fingerprints could collide if probe bytes are reordered in future
tasks.defer_reason: The probe byte arrays are compile-time constants and are never modified at runtime. The fingerprints correctly identify each probe today with no overlap. The finding is purely hypothetical future-breakage with no current failure scenario.

tasks.title: installProbeStubs stub never returns true for wasmExceptions probe, silently leaving wasmExceptions=false in all matrix tests
tasks.reason_for_tier: category 'dead branch / test gap' defaults to sonnet (standard local work)

tasks.title: beforeEach does not delete stale SharedArrayBuffer stub when originalSharedArrayBuffer was undefined
tasks.reason_for_tier: category 'wrong state-transition order' defaults to sonnet (standard local work)

tasks.title: detectTier has no assertion that its output is consistent with computeCapabilities selectedWasmBuild
tasks.reason_for_tier: category 'no invariant-checking convention' defaults to sonnet (standard local work)

tasks.title: canUseThreadedWasm is only tested with three of four boolean combinations
tasks.category: no property-based testing on math/state-heavy code
tasks.defer_reason: tier.test.ts line 15 explicitly tests canUseThreadedWasm(false, false) and asserts false. The (false,false) case IS covered in a different test file. The finding self-acknowledges 'all four combinations are actually covered' — this is not a real gap.

tasks.title: WebAssembly.validate probes run twice on first getCapabilities() call due to computeCapabilities calling probes then detectTier
tasks.reason_for_tier: category 'redundant computation' defaults to sonnet (standard local work)

tasks.title: Individual probe results (_probeSimd, _probeRelaxedSimd, etc.) are not cached at module level
tasks.reason_for_tier: category 'missing probe result caching' defaults to sonnet (standard local work)

tasks.title: probeWebGpuAdapter() crashes on Node.js: bare navigator access without guard
tasks.defer_reason: The bare navigator access is inside a try { ... } catch { return false; } block. A ReferenceError thrown in Node.js would be caught by the surrounding catch, returning false safely. This is not a crash — the catch block prevents it. The inconsistency with other navigator guards is a style concern, not a safety defect.

tasks.title: Capabilities.libjxlVersion is permanently 'unknown' despite a typed string contract
tasks.reason_for_tier: category 'serialization drift / misleading contract' defaults to sonnet (standard local work)

tasks.title: recommendedEffort() return type declares 1-9 but only 4, 6, or 7 are reachable
tasks.reason_for_tier: category 'type contract drift' defaults to sonnet (standard local work)

tasks.title: Capabilities.imageDecoder means 'class exists' not 'JXL is supported', conflating two distinct facts
tasks.reason_for_tier: category 'schema ambiguity / semantic gap' defaults to sonnet (standard local work)

tasks.title: canUseThreadedWasm() is exported but never called internally; detectTier() duplicates its logic inline
tasks.category: exported API dead code / internal duplication
tasks.defer_reason: detectTier() intentionally uses hasSab && (crossOriginIsolated || !isBrowser) which extends the predicate for Node.js. canUseThreadedWasm(sab, coi) encodes only the browser rule. These are not duplicates — they are different predicates for different contexts. Using canUseThreadedWasm internally in detectTier would be incorrect for Node.js environments.

tasks.title: detectTier() returns 'scalar' for both 'no WebAssembly' and 'WASM but no SIMD', undocumented in return type
tasks.defer_reason: The JSDoc comment at lines 84-88 explicitly documents this behavior: 'Returns scalar both when WebAssembly lacks SIMD and when WebAssembly is entirely absent; consumers that must distinguish should use getCapabilities().selectedWasmBuild'. The limitation is already documented — this is a documentation finding at most, not a contract defect.

tasks.title: No runtime schema validation: Capabilities object is never structurally verified before being returned
tasks.defer_reason: The Capabilities object is built in a single deterministic function where every field is explicitly set. TypeScript compile-time checking already enforces the return type. Runtime schema validation would be redundant for a small, closed function that does not accept external data. Adding zod/typebox would be an unnecessary dependency.

tasks.title: No test verifies that all fields of the Capabilities interface are present and typed correctly in the returned object
tasks.reason_for_tier: category 'missing contract testing' defaults to sonnet (standard local work)

tasks.title: _resetCache() races with in-flight computeCapabilities() producing two simultaneous probes
tasks.reason_for_tier: category 'TOCTOU / stale-promise after reset' defaults to sonnet (standard local work)

tasks.title: Test patches WebAssembly.validate on the shared global before an await without async-scoped isolation, exposing a window where concurrent test infrastructure reads the stub
tasks.reason_for_tier: category 'global mutation / missing async isolation in tests' defaults to sonnet (standard local work)
tasks.category: global mutation / missing async isolation in tests

tasks.reason_for_tier: severity 'high' + async reliability impact warrants opus review

tasks.title: probeWebGpuAdapter has no timeout — gpu.requestAdapter() can stall indefinitely

tasks.title: Dynamic import of @casabio/jxl-native swallows all errors including non-ModuleNotFound ones
tasks.reason_for_tier: category 'swallowed exception / unhandled rejection' defaults to sonnet (standard local work)

tasks.title: probeWebGpuAdapter accesses navigator without existence guard
tasks.defer_reason: The bare navigator access is inside a try { ... } catch { return false; } block. A ReferenceError thrown in Node.js would be caught by the surrounding catch, returning false safely. The catch block provides adequate protection — the function cannot crash. The inconsistency with the guarded pattern elsewhere is a style issue, not a safety defect.

tasks.title: ImageDecoder.isTypeSupported result is not narrowed before boolean coercion — a non-boolean truthy return goes unchecked
tasks.defer_reason: The WebCodecs spec explicitly defines isTypeSupported() as returning Promise<boolean>. The if (await ...) idiom is standard JavaScript boolean coercion. The concern about 'historically truthy values' is speculative without concrete browser evidence. The probe returning true on isTypeSupported=true without full instantiation is a known deliberate trade-off.

tasks.title: No structured diagnostic channel: all probe failures are silently swallowed with no observability hook
tasks.reason_for_tier: category 'no structured logging / error taxonomy' defaults to sonnet (standard local work)

tasks.title: No shared withTimeout utility — two async probes independently need timeout protection
tasks.reason_for_tier: category 'no default-timeout pattern' defaults to sonnet (standard local work)
